visa_types.name: Sweden E-application
visa_types.overview:
Initial Document Review by Submission Officer
- Your Sweden application should be submitted using the Sweden e-application portal.
- After the applicant uploads the required documents via the VFS online portal, the submission officer at VFS:
- Reviews the uploaded documents for completeness and accuracy.
- May request additional documents via the portal if anything is missing or unclear.
- Note: If a cancellation is requested after submission of the application on the online portal and the documents have been verified, only the Visa Fee will be refunded. The Service Fee becomes non-refundable once the documents are verified. Please check your details carefully before submission of your application.
Application Form Generation
- Once all required documents are accepted:
- The applicant receives a notification to download, sign, and re-upload the application form via the same online portal.
- This form is generated based on the submitted information.
- Once your application is submitted on the online portal and the document upload process is completed, you will:
- Be provided the option to book an appointment on the online portal so that you can give your biometrics at VFS and submit your passport or;
- If you have given your biometrics previously for a Schengen visa withing the last 59 months, you may mail-in your passport or drop your passport to VFS in person during opening hours (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/gbr/en/swe/attend-centre).
Note
- Biometrics given for a Residence permit or D visa or a refusal cannot be reused for a Schengen Visa.
- If a minor (customers under the age of 18) is applying, the customer must attend the VFS center with both the parents. Postal application will not be accepted in these cases.

Documents Required:
Please see here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Ucfr6NqHW8Drc1WZ5FxOt/0ab2ad5e3f367530745198af56950c6a/checklist.pdf) for the documents you will need to have ready for the e-submission of your application to VFS.

Processing time:
- Once your passport is received at VFS, it is dispatched to the Swedish Migration Agency in Sweden for processing. Note that international shipping can take documents 3-5 working days to reach Sweden and addition 3-5 working days, return to the UK.
- You can apply 6 months in advance for a Schengen visa and it is strongly advisable to apply as early as possible in advance so that you meet processing timelines and accommodate the transportation timeframes. During the peak season, please note that document check may take longer than usual.
- The application processing begins once the passport has physically reached the Swedish Migration Agency in Sweden. The minimum processing time for the application at the Swedish Migration Agency is 15 calendar days. The handling time may vary between 15 to 45 days, depending on different case related circumstances.
- Please also note that the Council of the European Union may impose visa measures against countries (nationalities), where the normal processing time is set to 45 days (Press releases and statements - Consilium) (https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/).
- The 15-calendar-day processing time begins only after the passport is received by the Swedish Migration Agency.
- The applicant can track progress via the VFS tracking tool or through notifications.
visa_types.full_text:
Initial Document Review by Submission Officer
- Your Sweden application should be submitted using the Sweden e-application portal.
- After the applicant uploads the required documents via the VFS online portal, the submission officer at VFS:
- Reviews the uploaded documents for completeness and accuracy.
- May request additional documents via the portal if anything is missing or unclear.
- Note: If a cancellation is requested after submission of the application on the online portal and the documents have been verified, only the Visa Fee will be refunded. The Service Fee becomes non-refundable once the documents are verified. Please check your details carefully before submission of your application.
Application Form Generation
- Once all required documents are accepted:
- The applicant receives a notification to download, sign, and re-upload the application form via the same online portal.
- This form is generated based on the submitted information.
- Once your application is submitted on the online portal and the document upload process is completed, you will:
- Be provided the option to book an appointment on the online portal so that you can give your biometrics at VFS and submit your passport or;
- If you have given your biometrics previously for a Schengen visa withing the last 59 months, you may mail-in your passport or drop your passport to VFS in person during opening hours (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/gbr/en/swe/attend-centre).
Note
- Biometrics given for a Residence permit or D visa or a refusal cannot be reused for a Schengen Visa.
- If a minor (customers under the age of 18) is applying, the customer must attend the VFS center with both the parents. Postal application will not be accepted in these cases.

Documents Required:
Please see here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Ucfr6NqHW8Drc1WZ5FxOt/0ab2ad5e3f367530745198af56950c6a/checklist.pdf) for the documents you will need to have ready for the e-submission of your application to VFS.

Processing time:
- Once your passport is received at VFS, it is dispatched to the Swedish Migration Agency in Sweden for processing. Note that international shipping can take documents 3-5 working days to reach Sweden and addition 3-5 working days, return to the UK.
- You can apply 6 months in advance for a Schengen visa and it is strongly advisable to apply as early as possible in advance so that you meet processing timelines and accommodate the transportation timeframes. During the peak season, please note that document check may take longer than usual.
- The application processing begins once the passport has physically reached the Swedish Migration Agency in Sweden. The minimum processing time for the application at the Swedish Migration Agency is 15 calendar days. The handling time may vary between 15 to 45 days, depending on different case related circumstances.
- Please also note that the Council of the European Union may impose visa measures against countries (nationalities), where the normal processing time is set to 45 days (Press releases and statements - Consilium) (https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/).
- The 15-calendar-day processing time begins only after the passport is received by the Swedish Migration Agency.
- The applicant can track progress via the VFS tracking tool or through notifications.

## Information for your visa application:
Do I require a visa (https://www.government.se/government-policy/migration-and-asylum/list-of-foreign-citizens-who-require-visa-for-entry-into-sweden/)
Information on visas (https://www.migrationsverket.se/English/Private-individuals/Visiting-Sweden/Visit-Sweden-up-to-90-days---apply-for-a-visa.html)
Documents required (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/Ucfr6NqHW8Drc1WZ5FxOt/0ab2ad5e3f367530745198af56950c6a/checklist.pdf)

If visiting family and friends in Sweden: Invitation – Before application for Schengen visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2MMIKVonbg8x4mzhMDj7op/199da5a1676559b2b5cbfd449f5ba2f3/9aaf3330-d5fb-4460-b992-86e07430515c.pdf)
Visa and Service fees (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7F91wR5Crvvm8uDIsp4p0/cb8b0aee16e7ddec6181827ef07e4863/Visa_and_Service_fees.pdf)
Photo Specifications (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2016-12/icao_photograph_guidelines_en.pdf) (Note, only JPG format is accepted, must be minimum 300 dpi)
How is my data processed by the Swedish Migration Agency (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5KuaqQ2MWV2gZYehlOVXXn/8c7bd34045259ed0932ff1fd60f958f6/GDPR-VFS.pdf)
FAQ
If you are ready to begin your application, please click here (https://seonline.vfsevisa.com/se/en/login) to begin.
